from source import find_in_sorted

def test_find_in_sorted_infinite_recursion_upper_half():
    """
    Trigger the infinite recursion bug when the target is greater than all elements.
    The buggy code uses `mid` as the lower bound for the upper half instead of `mid + 1`,
    causing the search interval to never shrink when the target lies in the upper half.
    """
    arr = [3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
    x = 8  # not present, greater than all elements
    # On buggy code this will raise RecursionError due to infinite recursion.
    # On fixed code it should return -1.
    result = find_in_sorted(arr, x)
    assert result == -1, "Expected -1 for target not found, buggy code causes infinite recursion"
